prompt: In our checkout service (Hoffmann.Checkout.Api), I introduced an `IPaymentGatewayClient` interface six months ago when we thought we'd swap from Stripe to Adyen. We never did. We still have only `StripePaymentGatewayClient` as the implementation. All ~12 callers use the interface. Should I rip it out? My junior keeps saying 'we should keep the abstraction in case we change later'. What's your take?
assertions: Applies the deletion test by name (as a named heading or explicit step)
States the two-adapter rule (one adapter = hypothetical/indirection, two = real seam)
Recommends deletion (conditional on real test-double adapter is acceptable)
Uses at least 3 of the skill vocabulary terms: seam, adapter, port, leverage, depth, locality, shallow
Names the anti-pattern explicitly (adding a port for one implementation OR 'in case we change later')
Does NOT propose new interface signatures or implementation code (workflow says discuss before designing)
Ends with 'Which of these would you like to explore?' or equivalent prompt to user
